cover_letter: December 1, 2025 Charles River Associates Recruiting Team Re: Application for Economics Consulting Analyst/Intern Summer 2026 Dear Charles River Associates Recruiting Team, I am excited to apply for the 2026 Economics Consulting Analyst/Intern role. I am a junior at the University of Michigan studying Economics with a minor in Statistics. Through rigorous coursework and research driven projects, I have built a strong foundation in structured problem solving and quantitative analysis. I have earned University Honors and recognition as a James B. Angell Scholar, and I am eager to bring that same intellectual rigor to Charles River Associates? collaborative consulting teams. This past summer at Aspen Academies, a private investment firm focused on acquiring and scaling early childcare companies, I worked on strategy and diligence for potential acquisitions. For one target set, the data we needed simply did not exist in any usable form. I designed a mystery shopping outreach process to collect operational and financial information directly from market participants, then organized and analyzed the results in Excel to inform valuation and fit. That experience reflects the kind of work I enjoy most: taking an ambiguous question, building a practical way to answer it, and communicating clear recommendations to senior stakeholders. I also bring client facing instincts and strong real time judgment from luxury retail roles at Alo and Brooks Brothers. At Alo, I supported the launch of a new store that generated over $30,000 in sales within its first three hours, adjusting sales strategy on the floor based on live customer patterns and performance metrics. Across both brands, I learned how to build rapport quickly, listen for what a customer actually needs, and deliver a tailored solution under pressure. Those are the same skills I want to apply with consulting clients. Outside of work, I serve as President of the University of Michigan Men?s Club Ice Hockey team, leading a 30 athlete organization and managing a $150,000 annual budget. Balancing team operations, recruiting, compliance, and high time commitment athletics with a full academic load has strengthened my ability to prioritize, lead through fast moving challenges, and keep groups aligned toward shared goals. Charles River Associates? focus on practical, data informed strategy and execution is exactly the environment where I believe I would thrive. I would love to contribute my analytical approach, resourcefulness, and people first working style to your team this summer.
extra_context: Strong consulting-interest student with strategy, analytics, and financial modeling exposure.
jobText: Associate (Intern - Summer 2026), Management Consulting. Location: New York, NY. Qualifications: Pursuit of a bachelor?s or master?s degree in a quantitative, problem solving, or critical reasoning discipline from a top educational institution. Preference will be given to upperclassmen and graduating students. Strong interest in and passion for the aviation industry. Analytical mindset with strong problem-solving skills. High level of motivation and drive for excellence. High level of focus on work quality and attention to detail. Comprehension of finance fundamentals and strong analytical skills. Proficiency in Microsoft Office products, particularly Microsoft Excel. Ability to perform under pressure and within tight deadlines. Desire to work in a multicultural, diverse, and team-oriented environment. Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English. Responsibilities: You will gain deeper insight into the aviation industry and the type of work that Alton's professionals engage in every day. Throughout your work with Alton, you will be working closely with Alton's experts to apply commercial expertise and rigorous analysis to solving real-world problems that aviation industry players are faced with. From day one, you will participate in tasks such as market research, data analysis, and building strategic recommendation reports. The internship will sharpen your understanding of business analytics and presentation tools, as well as equip you with in-depth knowledge on aviation market trends. Work as a member of a consulting team on client engagements to gather, analyze, and interpret data in support of hypothesis testing and solution development. Conduct market research, perform both quantitative and qualitative analyses including financial model development, and author presentations and reports.
